turns.content: Hey, I'm new to this codebase and I'm completely lost about how we run tests here. Every time I try to write a test it seems like everything just fails immediately or the compiler is giving me errors that make no sense.

Don't worry, that's totally normal when you first start. We use rstest for our tests so you can create test functions that look like regular functions but get executed by the framework. The key thing to understand is how we set up test data before each run using fixtures which are helper structs with Drop cleanup that handle setting up and tearing down the environment automatically.
Oh so I don't have to manually create all my test objects every time? Like if I'm testing a database connection or something?

Exactly! That's the whole point. You define a fixture struct that implements Drop so it cleans up resources when you're done with the test. Then in your actual tests you can just call setup methods on that fixture to get everything ready. This way you only write the logic once and reuse it across parametrized tests or different scenarios without duplicating code.
